purpose: Test docs/alm_consciousness_joint_matrix_20260425.md §9 hypothesis: 'lasttoken reduction이 Δ_pres / Δ_des 분리도를 회복할 수 있음'. BASE p1 only (raw#9 hexa-only — TRAINED lasttoken artifact 부재, 새 forward 필요).
method: Run tool/an11_b_v3_cps.hexa on two BASE p1 h_last_raw variants sharing the same 16-prompt set + same hidden_dim (256) + same model (Qwen3-8B-Base null), differing only in reduction op.
hypothesis_finding: Both reductions yield CPS ≈ 0.93 with δ_preserve > δ_destruct (CPS < 1.0). lasttoken does NOT recover Δ_pres/Δ_des separation at BASE p1 — it is fractionally lower (CPS −0.006, more anti-discriminating). Reduction op is NOT the source of V3 signal absence at BASE.
interpretation: preserve_permutation (EN↔KO swap) induces a larger Gram Frobenius disturbance than destruct_permutation (random) under both reductions. This means the row-order-sensitive Gram metric ranks 'semantic preserve' as more disruptive than 'semantic destruct' — opposite of V3 spec intent. The phenomenon is reduction-invariant at BASE, indicating the surrogate metric (Gram Frobenius distance) does not align with V3's stated semantic-discrimination goal on the current 16-prompt set + EN↔KO permutation pair.
scope_limit: BASE only. TRAINED lasttoken artifact does not exist — extrapolation to TRAINED requires new forward (raw#9 hexa-only violation, H100 launch needed). However, BASE evidence already falsifies the §9 hypothesis at the substrate level: if reduction op were the lever, swapping it on the same model should shift CPS materially; it does not.
next_step_recommendation: Archive V3 lift target (lasttoken reduction) as ineffective. Consider (a) different perturbation pair design (e.g., destruct_permutation that genuinely breaks semantic blocks rather than random shuffle which preserves block diagonal in some seeds), or (b) replace Gram Frobenius with a true semantic metric (e.g., per-prompt cosine vs ground-truth EN↔KO pair), or (c) Mk.IX L_IX architecture change. NOT a fine-tune lever.
